contact_lookup_status: No named recruiter or contact was visible on the live Vishay Workday posting checked on 2026-04-22, so the letter uses Hiring Team as the fallback.
why_company: What caught my attention about Vishay is that this role is concrete in the right way: support real controlling operations, help with month-end and quarter-end work, keep SAP master data reliable, and prepare analyses and presentations that other stakeholders can actually use. I am especially interested in roles where structured analytical work improves day-to-day business decisions.
job_description: Vishay Semiconductor GmbH is hiring a working student in Controlling in Heilbronn. The role supports general administrative work in the controlling function, master-data maintenance in SAP, support for monthly and quarterly closing activities, and preparation of ad hoc evaluations, analyses, and presentations for different stakeholders. The posting asks for a currently enrolled student in business administration, preferably with a controlling focus, a structured and analytical mindset, hands-on attitude, independent and careful working style, confident use of MS Office especially Excel, initial SAP R/3 experience as a plus, and good German and English skills.
requirements: Currently enrolled student in business administration, preferably with a focus on controlling
Structured and analytical way of thinking
Hands-on attitude and careful, independent, goal-oriented working style
Confident use of MS Office applications, especially Excel
Initial experience with SAP R/3 is desirable
Ability to support monthly and quarterly closing work
Ability to prepare ad hoc evaluations, analyses, and presentations for stakeholders
cv_about_override: Business analysis and operations-oriented professional focused on structured reporting, stakeholder-ready analysis, and practical support for finance and operational workflows.
cv_summary_override: Experienced business analysis and product professional with a strong foundation in structured execution, stakeholder alignment, documentation, workflow improvement, and practical analytical thinking. Through Software Engineering studies at Heilbronn University, Business Information Systems studies at Vietnamese-German University, and hands-on work across SQL, reporting-oriented analysis, automation, and digital operations, I have been building toward roles that combine structured analysis, reliable reporting support, and stakeholder-facing operational coordination.
cover_letter_opening: I wanted to apply for the Werkstudent (m/w/d) Controlling role at Vishay because the work itself is concrete and useful: support controlling operations, help with SAP master data, contribute to monthly and quarterly closing work, and prepare analyses and presentations that help stakeholders make decisions.
cover_letter_body_one: Across business analysis, product delivery, and operations-heavy environments, I have often worked where teams needed clearer reporting, stronger documentation, and more structured ways to turn operational information into actionable next steps. That has made me comfortable with careful analysis, stakeholder-facing materials, and reliable day-to-day support work.
cover_letter_body_two: What makes this role especially relevant for me is the combination of analysis, disciplined execution, and practical business support. Through my Business Information Systems background, my current Software Engineering studies at Heilbronn University, and my hands-on work across SQL, structured reporting, and cross-functional coordination, I have been building toward exactly this kind of role: one where careful analysis and dependable follow-through matter just as much as technical curiosity.
cover_letter_motivation: I am particularly interested in Vishay because the company combines a strong industrial technology environment with a very practical student role close to real business operations in Heilbronn.
cover_letter_closing: I am available to start from 06 May 2026 and can work up to 20 hours per week in line with my current student visa. I work confidently in English and continue to strengthen my German. I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how I can support your controlling team.
